name: Take-me-to-a-Picnic Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Grease and flour a 15-1/2 x 10-1/2 x 1-inch jellyroll pan.
3. In a medium saucepan, combine water, butter, and cocoa. Cook over medium heat, stirring occasionally until the mixture boils. Boil for 1 minute, then remove from heat and set aside.
4. In a large mixing bowl, stir together sugar, flour, baking soda, and salt. Add eggs and sour cream; beat until blended.
5. Add the cocoa mixture to the bowl; beat just until blended. The batter will be thin. Pour into the prepared pan.
6. Bake in a 350°F oven for 25 to 30 minutes or until a wooden pick inserted into the center comes out clean. Cool cake on a wire rack.
7. Spread Peanut Butter Chip Frosting over the top of the cake. Drizzle Chocolate Garnish over the top, if desired.
Peanut Butter Chip Frosting:
1/3 cup Butter
1/3 cup Milk
10 oz Peanut Butter Chips
1 tsp Vanilla Extract
1 cup Powdered Sugar
1. In a medium saucepan, combine butter, milk, and peanut butter chips. Cook over low heat, stirring constantly until the chips are melted and the mixture is smooth.
2. Remove from heat; stir in vanilla extract.
3. In a small mixing bowl, place powdered sugar. Gradually add the chip mixture, beating until well blended.
Chocolate Garnish:
1/2 cup Semisweet Chocolate Chips
1 teaspoon Shortening
1. Place chocolate chips and shortening in a small microwave-safe bowl. Cook on high for 1 minute, stirring until melted and smooth. Do not use butter or margarine.
2. This garnish makes about 20 servings.
